summary: Joel briefly highlights complaints from Jeff Jarvis and Robert Scoble about the conference industry. Jarvis claims too many conferences are poor quality, while Scoble points out that scaling conferences to 1,000+ attendees causes costs to rise exponentially due to convention center price gouging. The post is essentially a link roundup with minimal original commentary.
key_points: Many conferences deliver poor value to attendees
Convention center costs scale poorly, with absurd markups like $1000 for an urn of coffee
The conference business model may be fundamentally broken at scale
money_quotes: Costs per attendee start going up exponentially.
key_insight: The conference industry suffers from both quality problems and perverse cost structures that punish scale.
